title: Модель выполнения CLR: исходник → IL → JIT → машинный код

cards.prompt: A CLR compiler emits a managed module = a real PE file on disk (not native code). The type StringBuilder resolves to assembly System.Private.CoreLib whose module file has extension .dll, so the concatenation prints System.Private.CoreLib.dll
cards.code: using System; using System.Text; var a = typeof(StringBuilder).Assembly; Console.WriteLine(a.GetName().Name + System.IO.Path.GetExtension(a.Location));

cards.prompt: The compiler emits IL, not machine code: Add(a,b)=>a+b compiles to a 4-byte IL body ldarg.0 (2) ldarg.1 (3) add (88=0x58) ret (42=0x2A), so GetILAsByteArray prints 4: 2 3 88 42
cards.code: using System; using System.Reflection; class C { public static int Add(int a,int b)=>a+b; } var il = typeof(C).GetMethod("Add").GetMethodBody().GetILAsByteArray(); Console.WriteLine($"{il.Length}: {string.Join(" ", il)}");

cards.prompt: The CLR loads assemblies, not raw modules; a single-file C# assembly groups exactly one module. System.Private.CoreLib has GetModules().Length == 1, so it prints System.Private.CoreLib / 1
cards.code: using System; using System.Text; var a = typeof(StringBuilder).Assembly; Console.WriteLine($"{a.GetName().Name} / {a.GetModules().Length}");
